data: re-classify salary_type with URL fetch + improved algorithm

Improved approach: fetch actual job posting text → LLM reads real words
instead of guessing from salary range size.

Fixed 3 false positives (total_comp → base):
- Owner: Senior Software Engineer, Full-Stack (posting says "base salary range ... plus equity")
- Waabi: Software Engineer
- Achievers: Staff Software Engineer

New distribution: base(43) / total_comp(0) — correct for Canadian market.
Rules now: only total_comp if explicitly bundled; "base + separate equity" = base.

commit.message:
Restore block-dangerous-commands.sh hook

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(gateway): resolve system audit fields in ?include=users (#452)

The System Information section on detail pages showed dashes for
Created By, Updated By, Created, and Updated because the
SubResourceResolver only checked explicit field definitions when
resolving ?include=users. Since created_by/updated_by are system
audit columns not present in field definitions, the resolver never
found the relationship and the users data was omitted from the
API response.

Add implicit createdBy/updatedBy → users relationships in
resolveAll() so the include resolution picks them up.

Co-authored-by: Craig Klinker <craigklinker@velocityglobal.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(stitcher): use -c copy for trimming — libx264 hangs in FFmpeg WASM

libx264 re-encoding hangs indefinitely in FFmpeg WASM on Kling-generated
clips, causing the stitch to silently freeze at 45%. Switch all trim
steps to -c copy (keyframe-aligned cuts, no re-encode). FFmpeg WASM
handles stream copy reliably; the final concat -c copy still produces
a valid MP4 since all three clips share the same codec from the same
source.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- NORMAL(불장): v1 파라미터 (SL=ATR×0.5, TP=ATR×1.6, 전량청산)
- RECOVERY(회복장): v2 파라미터 (SL=ATR×0.7, TP=ATR×2.0, 반분할)

commit.message:
feat(epic-280B): automation entities, enums, sealed configs & persistence tests (#556)

* feat(epic-280B): automation entities, enums, sealed configs & persistence tests

- 7 enums: TriggerType, ActionType, ExecutionStatus, ActionExecutionStatus,
  RuleSource, DelayUnit, ConditionOperator
- 4 entities: AutomationRule, AutomationAction, AutomationExecution,
  ActionExecution (all with JSONB columns)
- 4 JPA repositories with custom query methods
- Sealed class hierarchies: TriggerConfig (3 permits), ActionConfig (6 permits),
  ActionResult (2 permits)
- ConfigDeserializer utility for type-safe JSONB deserialization
- 12 persistence integration tests covering CRUD, relationships, JSONB round-trips,
  enum storage, status transitions, and config deserialization

feat: add sub-trait support for sanely-jsoniter (#104)

Sealed trait variants that are themselves sealed traits are now
flattened — encoding produces {"LeafName": {...}} instead of
{"SubTraitName": {"LeafName": {...}}}. Handles arbitrary depth
and diamond inheritance with dedup. Works in both unconfigured
and configured derivation.

Now only ASCII printable characters and above are passed to TextInput
for IME processing. Control characters (Enter, Tab, Escape, arrows)
are still handled directly by the terminal.

commit.message:
fix(resilience): Prevent overflow in exponential backoff calculation

Cap attempt count to 20 before exponentiation to prevent OverflowError
when attempt numbers get very large. The delay is still correctly capped
at max_delay (300s default). This prevents 500 errors from being returned
to users due to CIRISLens SDK errors.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Eliminate name form flash: batch room + fingerprint into single state update

Previously fetchRoom() and the fingerprint check resolved independently,
causing multiple render transitions. Now both run in parallel via
Promise.all and all state is set in one batch — the component goes
directly from "Loading room..." to the final view with no intermediate
render of the name form.

feat: migrate from electron to tauri (#37)

- Add src-tauri/ with Cargo.toml, Rust entry points, and shell detection commands
- Add tauri.conf.json with frameless window, CSP, and capabilities config
- Add TauriTerminal component (xterm.js + tauri-plugin-pty + WebGL)
- Add TauriTitleBar with Tauri window API (minimize/maximize/close/drag)
- Add TauriApp entry point with show-on-ready pattern
- Add vite.config.tauri.ts for dual-build (Electron + Tauri side-by-side)
- Add tauri-index.html as separate entry for Tauri build
- Add Tauri npm deps (@tauri-apps/api, tauri-pty, @tauri-apps/plugin-os)
- Add dev:tauri and build:tauri scripts to package.json
- Update .gitignore for dist-tauri/ and src-tauri/target/
- All 653 existing tests pass, both typechecks pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve code review findings in Tauri POC (F1-F5)

- F1 [HIGH]: Store PTY event handler disposables, unlisten on cleanup
- F2 [MEDIUM]: Track WebGL addon in ref, dispose explicitly, check disposed flag
- F3 [MEDIUM]: Fix race condition in TauriTitleBar resize listener with mounted flag
- F4 [MEDIUM]: Add disposedRef guard to ResizeObserver, fix cleanup order
- F5 [MEDIUM]: Validate PATH-based shells on Windows using 'where' command

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add url config to tauri window for production build

Tauri defaults to loading index.html but our entry is tauri-index.html.
Without explicit url config, the frontend never loads and window stays hidden.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(tauri): complete Fase 4 - add backup/rollback adapters and NSIS config

AC-10: Implement backup and rollback Tauri adapters
- Add tauri-backup-api.ts with createBackup, listBackups, restoreBackup, cleanupOldBackups
- Add tauri-rollback-api.ts with keepPreviousVersion, availableRollbacks, installRollback

AC-12: Update README with Tauri build instructions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: spam terminal

* fix: add isTauriContext guards to prevent browser crash

Without these guards, getCurrentWindow() throws TypeError in plain
browser because window.__TAURI_INTERNALS__ is undefined. This crashed
the entire React app via error boundary when TitleBar.tsx called
windowApi.onMaximizeChange() in a useEffect.

- tauri-window-api.ts: add isTauriContext() helper, guard all methods
  (minimize, toggleMaximize, close, onMaximizeChange, onCloseRequested,
  respondToClose) to return early/noop when not in Tauri context
- TauriApp.tsx: guard showWindow() call with isTauriContext check
- TitleBar.tsx: related cleanup from guard addition

* fix: defer initial fitAddon.fit() to requestAnimationFrame

WebglAddon.activate() is async internally. Calling fitAddon.fit()
synchronously right after loadWebglAddon() caused an uncaught:
  TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'dimensions')
5 times on startup (once per terminal), sourced from xterm's
RenderService.get dimensions() -> this._renderer.value.dimensions
where _renderer.value is undefined until WebGL finishes initializing.

Fix: wrap initial fit() call in requestAnimationFrame() so the WebGL
renderer has one frame tick to complete initialization.

Also fixes debounce test regression: add mockClear() after spawn
assertion to account for the needsResizeOnReadyRef immediate-resize
path that fires before the xterm onResize debounce under test.

* perf: reduce PTY kill timeout from 2000ms to 300ms

Reduces perceived lag when closing a terminal tab. The kill operation
should be near-instant for graceful shutdown; 300ms is a sufficient
safety margin for the race to avoid hanging the close flow.

* refactor(tauri): consolidate GitStatus type to shared IPC contract

- Re-export GitStatus from @shared/types/ipc.types in project.ts
- Update use-git-status.ts to import from shared types
- Ensures type consistency between Rust backend and renderer
- Eliminates duplicate GitStatus definition

This aligns the renderer domain model with the Tauri IPC contract,
ensuring the GitStatus interface matches the Rust tracker's serialized
output (camelCase, same fields).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(shared): add canonical Migration API contract

Define canonical MigrationApi interface with 6 methods:
- getVersion, getSchemaInfo, getHistory, getRegistered, runMigration, rollback
- Add IpcResult<T> type definitions for error handling
- Document Rust command naming mapping (snake_case)

This contract is used by both renderer adapters and Rust backend.

* feat(rust): implement data migration backend with store plugin

- Add MigrationManager with Tauri store plugin integration
- Implement data_migration_get_registered: return actual migrations
- Implement data_migration_run_migrations: process pending migrations
- Implement data_migration_rollback: execute rollback to version
- Register MigrationManager in app state

Replaces placeholder implementations with working backend.

* fix(rust): fix CWD and Git tracker polling not starting

- Fix ensure_polling_started() implementation (was empty)
- Add interior mutability pattern (Arc<RwLock<Option<JoinHandle>>>)
- Add is_polling_started atomic flag for proper one-time initialization
- Add poll_count indicator for testability
- Fix shutdown() to properly abort tasks

Closes: CWD changes were never detected, terminal-cwd-changed event never fired

* feat(renderer): add Tauri adapters for Session and Data Migration

- Implement SessionApi interface using @tauri-apps/plugin-store
- Implement MigrationApi interface with canonical contract
- Map invoke calls to Rust commands (snake_case)
- Handle IpcResult<T> success/error responses

These adapters complete the Tauri implementation for Session and Data Migration domains.

* refactor(renderer): make facade Tauri-first with Electron compatibility

- Add isTauriContext() guard using window.__TAURI_INTERNALS__
- Export sessionApi from Tauri adapter on Tauri runtime
- Export dataMigrationApi from Tauri adapter on Tauri runtime
- Mark session-api.ts and data-migration-api.ts as deprecated (Electron only)
- Add explicit error boundaries for wrong context usage

BREAKING-CHANGE: Session and Data Migration APIs now route to Tauri
by default. Electron fallback available via compatibility modules.

* docs(renderer): document keyboard and system event fallback mode

- Document Fallback Mode decision for keyboard events (WebView2 limitation)
- Document Heartbeat approach for system power-resume (chosen, not workaround)
- Add TODO for what would be needed for Backend Event Mode
- Reference decision document from keyboard/system APIs

Decision: Fallback Mode over Backend Event Mode due to technical limitations.
See: docs/decisions/keyboard-system-event-mode-final.md

* feat(renderer): implement bootstrap context detection and visibility integration

- Add isTauriContext() detection in main.tsx bootstrap
- Route to TauriApp component when Tauri context detected
- Integrate visibility state with backend terminal_set_visibility command
- Ensure visibility-aware polling works end-to-end

Enables CPU-saving pause/resume behavior for trackers when app hidden.

* test(renderer): add test suite for Session, Data Migration, and parity

- Add 18 automated parity checks (Implemented/Wired/Verified per domain)
- Test Session adapter with all 5 methods (save, restore, clear, flush, hasSession)
- Test Data Migration adapter with canonical contract (rollback payload shape)
- Test facade routing uses Tauri adapters on Tauri runtime
- Add visibility transition and recovery scenarios

Covers regression prevention for Tauri-first facade pattern.

* test(main): add Electron IPC compatibility tests

- Test session:save, session:restore, session:clear, session:flush, session:hasSession
- Test dataMigration:runMigrations, dataMigration:rollback, etc.
- Verify IpcResult pattern compatibility between Electron and Tauri
- Validate legacy vs canonical method name mappings

Ensures Electron compatibility path continues working during coexistence.

* chore: update supporting types and preload interfaces

- Sync preload TypeScript definitions with Tauri API changes
- Update project types for new migration domain types
- Minor main process adjustments for compatibility

No functional changes, type sync only.

* feat: add logging support for terminal events and improve PTY management

* feat: enhance PTY management with dedicated writer and improve error handling in IPC tests

* feat: add various icon assets for Tauri application including adaptive icons and SVG

* feat: update Tauri configuration and dependencies, add MCP bridge plugin

* feat: add Git Bash path candidates and improve shell resolution on Windows

feat: configurable audio during fast-forward (#178)

* feat: configurable audio during fast-forward

Add a toggle in Settings > Emulation to play game audio at accelerated
speed during fast-forward (default: off). Previously audio was always
silenced during fast-forward. The setting is persisted to localStorage
and sent to the emulation worker via IPC so it takes effect immediately.

Also adds a future TODO in DEVELOPMENT_PLAN.md for a retro cozy
fast-forward audio effect (warm tape-whir hum, lo-fi crackle, low-pass
filtered game audio).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: reference #177 in dev plan for retro cozy FF audio effect

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: swallow errors from initial setFastForwardAudio IPC call

The worker may not be ready when game:loaded fires. Catch and ignore
errors from the fire-and-forget IPC call to prevent unhandled promise
rejections.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace empty catch with void to satisfy no-empty-function lint rule

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: deny media permission to prevent macOS microphone dialogs

AudioContext playback (audio output) does not require microphone/camera
access. The previous handler auto-approved all permissions including
'media', which caused Chromium to request microphone access from macOS,
triggering repeated system permission dialogs. Deny 'media' permission
specifically since we only play audio, never capture it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace remote image URLs in task markdown with local attachments (#74)

* fix: replace remote image URLs in task markdown with local attachments

Remote image links from Linear, Notion, and HubSpot expire over time,
causing images to stop rendering in task descriptions. After downloading
attachments locally, we now rewrite those URLs to use a custom
app-attachment:// protocol that serves files from the local disk.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve typecheck errors and deduplicate URL replacement logic

- Remove unused readFileSync import from index.ts
- Remove TOCTOU existsSync check in protocol handler (let readdirSync throw)
- Extract replaceRemoteImageUrlsInTask() helper to eliminate copy-paste across 3 plugins
- Fix TS2352 cast error by using Record<string, unknown> with unknown intermediate cast
- Change replaceRemoteImageUrls param type to Record<string, unknown>[] for flexibility

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use pathToFileURL to properly encode spaces in attachment file paths

The userData path on macOS contains "Application Support" (with a space),
and filenames can also have spaces/parens. Using template string file://
URLs left these unencoded, causing net.fetch to fail with broken images.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: allow app-attachment:// URLs in Markdown renderer

ReactMarkdown's defaultUrlTransform only allows http, https, mailto,
and xmpp protocols. Our custom app-attachment:// URLs were being
stripped to empty strings, causing broken images. Add a custom
urlTransform that passes through app-attachment:// URLs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
refactor(xlabmgmt): switch from GNOME to Niri for faster builds

Replace GNOME desktop environment with Niri scrollable-tiling compositor
to dramatically reduce build times and eliminate heavy dependencies.

Changes:
- Remove GNOME, GDM, gnome-disks, and gnome-tweaks
- Add Niri compositor with complete Wayland ecosystem
- Add fuzzel (app launcher), waybar (status bar), mako (notifications)
- Add essential tools: foot terminal, thunar file manager
- Keep Chrome and Firefox
- Preserve audio disabled setting (no audio hardware)
- Keep gnome-disk-utility (works without full GNOME)

Benefits:
- Expected build time: ~15-20 min (down from 4+ hours)
- Eliminates webkitgtk and heavy GNOME dependencies
- Lightweight, scrollable UI

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove hardcoded API key fallbacks from app.py

Keys must come from st.secrets only — no plaintext fallbacks in source.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(M2): WebSocket push, step batching, interactive parameters dashboard

Step batching (fixes 288× crapout):
- _sim_loop() now runs `batch = round(speed_mult * 0.05 / dt)` steps per
  tick (e.g. 29 steps at 288×) before sleeping, instead of one step at a
  time. Reduces lock acquisitions 576→20/s, keeps NumPy arrays hot in cache.

WebSocket (replaces HTTP polling):
- GET /ws — RFC 6455 handshake inline in existing ThreadingTCPServer.
- Server broadcasts state JSON after every batch; clients auto-reconnect.
- Green/red WS status dot in the UI header.
- HTTP GET /state kept as fallback.

Extended /control API (M2):
- POST /control now accepts optional "params" dict forwarded to
  SimConfig.update() — covers all IDM, MOBIL, signal, demand, weather
  and speed_mult fields in one call.

Interactive Parameters dashboard (viz/index.html):
- New "Parameters" card (bottom-right) with collapsible Driving /
  Signals / Demand / Weather sections.
- Preset buttons: Rush Hour, Aggressive, Rainy Day, Night, Reset.
- All sliders debounced 150 ms → POST /control {params: {...}}.
- Values persisted to localStorage and re-applied on page load.

Tests (23 new, 122 total — all pass):
- TestSimConfig: defaults, update, type coercion, thread-safety,
  snapshot, weather multipliers.
- TestConfigInSim: a_max propagated to spawned vehicles, weather slows
  fleet, config attached as sim.config.
- TestPresets: all preset keys exist in SimConfig.
- TestControlAPI: pause, speed_mult, params idm/weather/unknown/speed.
- TestWebSocket: 101 handshake, frame content, multiple frames,
  simultaneous reconnect.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(scene,parsing): add obj_count tracking and get_object_by_idx helper

Track scene->obj_count in add_object for index-based object selection.
Initialise mode/selected_idx in init_scene; add get_object_by_idx to
support Phase 10 bonus interaction features (mode switching, object
selection, camera/light/object transforms).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix register 500: remove is_phone_verified from INSERT, add schema patches

The Supabase users table was created before is_phone_verified was added,
causing PGRST204 (column not in schema cache). Fix:
- Remove explicit is_phone_verified:False from INSERT (use DB default FALSE)
- Add ALTER TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for all potentially missing user columns
  (is_phone_verified, push_token, state, country)

User must run the migration SQL in Supabase SQL Editor to add columns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: streamline Claude commands — 8 commands → 5, reduce friction (#43)

- Delete check.md (redundant with ship), review-pr.md (self-review
  ceremony), commit.md (absorbed into ship)
- Rename start-work → start, new-issue → issue, complete-issue → done
- New ship.md: stage+lint-autofix+test+commit+push+PR+board in one command
- fix.md: remove push/lint-fix confirmations, infer issue from branch
- done.md: infer issue from branch, remove post-merge smoke tests
- issue.md: default Priority=Medium Size=M, parse overrides from args
- _project-board.md: update command references to new names
- Confirmations reduced from 6 to 1 (merge only)

Simplify error pages: clear buttons, fix 419 refresh, remove confusing feature list

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite as per-source filter with GPU shader zoom

Instead of a global script controlling one source, this is now an OBS
filter that can be applied to any video source independently. Each
source gets its own zoom/pan settings and hotkeys.

Uses a custom GPU shader that remaps UV coordinates for the zoom —
no crop/scale filter hacks. At zoom 1.0 it's a clean passthrough.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
